name: Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón
name_original: Embalse de Alarcón – running circular desde Olmedilla de Alarcón
region_intro: Jogging routes around Manchuela traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, offering varied terrain for runners. The region features expansive crop fields, vineyards, and rolling hills, interspersed with coniferous forests and *dehesas*. Runners can explore routes along reservoirs like Alarcón and through dramatic river gorges, providing a mix of flat plains and undulating paths. This blend of natural features creates an engaging environment for outdoor activity. Best jogging routes around Manchuela The most popular jogging route is Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular from Alarcón, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Alarcón reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the reservoir and the open plains. Local runners also love the Running loop from Villamalea, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through agricultural fields and local countryside, often completed in about 25 minutes. Jogging around Manchuela is defined by riverine environments, expansive vineyards, and rolling hills, with routes often circling large reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops through fields to more challenging trails with varied elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 149 runners have used komoot to explore Manchuela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Manchuela?
A: Manchuela offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These trails explore the region's unique landscapes, from river valleys to expansive vineyards.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Manchuela?
A: Spring is an ideal time for running in Manchuela. The mild temperatures and vibrant countryside, rich with colors, create a particularly inviting environment for outdoor activity.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Manchuela?
A: Yes, Manchuela has several running options perfect for beginners. There are at least 3 easy routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villamalea&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.1 km (2.5 miles) trail that takes about 25 minutes to complete through agricultural fields.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Manchuela?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more vigorous run, Manchuela features 2 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014657455/sendero-de-la-hoz-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero de la Hoz de Alarcón – running circular from Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, a 11.4 km (7.1 miles) trail with significant elevation changes, offering stunning views of the Alarcón reservoir.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Manchuela?
A: Many of Manchuela's running trails are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014675865/embalse-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de la Pradera loop from El Picazo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on Manchuela's running trails?
A: Manchuela offers a rich tapestry of landscapes. You'll encounter expansive crop fields, vineyards, almond, olive, and pistachio trees, rolling hills, and coniferous forests. The region is also defined by dramatic riverine environments, particularly along the Júcar and Cabriel rivers, with impressive gorges and serene reservoir views.

Q: Are there running routes along rivers or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the Júcar and Cabriel rivers are central to Manchuela's natural beauty, and many routes follow their courses. You can also find trails circling significant water bodies like the Alarcón reservoir, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014675865/embalse-de-alarcon-running-circular-from-olmedilla-de-alarcon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – running circular from Olmedilla de Alarcón&lt;/a&gt;, providing serene waterside jogs.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: While specific running routes are not detailed to pass directly through all historical sites, the region is rich in history. For instance, the historic town of Alcalá del Júcar, with its unique setting and castle, is a focal point for many outdoor activities. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Castle&lt;/a&gt; is also a prominent landmark near some running trails.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in Manchuela?
A: Many of the easier and moderate routes through flatter plains and agricultural fields are suitable for families. The varied terrain ensures there are options for different paces and ages, allowing families to enjoy a run together. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' for the best family experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Manchuela?
A: Manchuela's natural trails are generally welcoming to dogs, especially those that traverse open fields and natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural lands, and to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas.

Q: What makes Manchuela a unique place for runners?
A: Manchuela's uniqueness for runners lies in its blend of diverse natural landscapes—from dramatic river gorges and serene reservoirs to expansive vineyards and rolling hills. The region offers a constantly changing visual experience, combined with a network of well-documented trails that cater to all fitness levels, making every run an adventure.

Q: Are there running routes with places to explore or points of interest?
A: Yes, several areas offer points of interest. For example, the Júcar River gorge near Alcalá del Júcar provides dramatic scenery. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or explore areas with unique geological formations like the Chorreras del Cabriel. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4475740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turia-Cabriel Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers sections for exploration.

Q: How can I find parking near the running trails?
A: Many running routes in Manchuela start from towns or villages, where street parking or designated public parking areas are typically available. For trails in more remote natural areas, look for small parking lots or pull-offs near trailheads. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific starting points and potential parking information.
tips: The path along the shore of the Alarcón reservoir is a beautiful route that allows you to enjoy impressive views of the reservoir and its natural surroundings. Approximately 9 km in total.

waypoints.name: Trail along the shore of the Alarcón reservoir
